pages.name: Home
pages.description: Landing page introducing the engineer and highlighting key sections of the portfolio. It surfaces the profile summary, primary specializations, featured DevOps/Software projects, and recent blog posts as entry points into deeper content.
primary_functions: Introduce the engineers profile, specializations (Software, DevOps, SRE) and value proposition.
Provide prominent links to Projects, Skills, Blog, Resume, and Contact sections.
Showcase a few featured projects or recent blog posts as entry points into deeper content.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'View all projects' CTA or Projects teaser section
Click on a featured project card
Click on 'View all posts' CTA or Blog teaser section
Click on a recent blog post card
Click on 'Generate tailored resume' CTA
Click on 'Learn more about me' link
Click on 'Get in touch' CTA

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry 'Home'
Can be accessed directly via root URL or index.html

pages.name: Projects
pages.description: Projects listing page for browsing and filtering all portfolio projects. Supports rich filtering (DevOps, Kubernetes, CI/CD, Terraform, AWS, environments, languages) and sorting to help recruiters quickly find relevant case studies and prepare for comparison or contact.
primary_functions: Display a list of project cards with key metadata (title, category, technologies, environment, dates).
Filter projects by category (e.g., DevOps), technologies/tags (e.g., Kubernetes, CI/CD, Terraform), languages, cloud provider (e.g., AWS), and environment (e.g., Production).
Search projects by keyword (e.g., 'microservices') and restrict results by language (e.g., Go, Node.js).
Sort projects by criteria such as last updated, start date (newest first), or relevance.
Open individual project details by clicking on a project card.
Mark/unmark projects for comparison via an 'Add to comparison' control and navigate to the comparison view using a 'Compare projects' button.

incoming_params.description: Optional search query used to pre-filter projects when arriving from another page.
Optional project category slug (e.g., 'devops') used to pre-select a category filter.
Comma-separated list of technology names or slugs (e.g., 'kubernetes,ci_cd') used to pre-select technology filters.
Comma-separated list of backend language keys (e.g., 'go,node_js') used to pre-filter by language.
Optional cloud provider key (e.g., 'aws') used to pre-select the cloud provider filter.
Optional environment key (e.g., 'production') used to pre-filter by deployment environment.
Optional sort key (e.g., 'last_updated_desc', 'start_date_desc') used to initialize the current sort.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on any project card
Click on 'Compare projects' button after selecting projects for comparison

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry 'Projects'
Links from Home, About, or Blog may point directly to the Projects listing
Accessible with optional filter/search parameters as query string values

pages.name: Project Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single project including context, stack, and outcomes. Used heavily by recruiters to understand scope, CI/CD practices, Kubernetes/Terraform usage, and to record the project as 'most relevant' in the evaluation notes.
primary_functions: Display the full project title as the main heading along with detailed description, responsibilities, technologies, and results.
Provide a 'Copy title' control next to the project heading to copy the full project title text.
Allow the project to be added to or removed from the comparison list via an 'Add to comparison' toggle.
Expose a 'Recruiter notes' or 'Evaluation' panel (e.g., slide-out from sidebar or header) with a 'Most relevant project' field where the copied title can be pasted and saved.
Offer navigation back to the projects listing or to other related projects.

incoming_params.description: Project.id of the project to display in detail.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Back to projects' link or breadcrumb
Click on 'View comparison' link/button when the project is part of the comparison selection

access_methods.description: Accessed via project_detail.html?id=<Project.id>
Linked from Projects listing, Home featured projects, or Project Comparison cells

pages.name: Project Comparison
pages.description: Side-by-side comparison view for selected projects, especially Terraform-on-AWS production work. Shows structured attributes for quick evaluation of recent infrastructure projects.
primary_functions: Display two or more selected projects side-by-side showing key attributes such as start date, environment (e.g., Production), cloud provider (e.g., AWS), infrastructure tools (e.g., Terraform), responsibilities, and outcomes.
Help users compare the two most recent Terraform-on-AWS production projects ordered by start date.
Provide links back to each projects detail page for deeper inspection.
Offer navigation back to the main projects listing to adjust filters or selection.

incoming_params.description: Optional sort key (e.g., 'start_date_desc') indicating how comparison projects were ordered when selected.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a project title or 'View details' link within the comparison table
Click on 'Back to projects' link

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'Compare projects' button from Projects listing or Project Detail pages

pages.name: Skills
pages.description: Skills overview page with filtering and shortlist functionality. Enables recruiters to quickly identify AWS DevOps/Cloud strengths and build a shortlist of key capabilities.
primary_functions: List skills grouped or filterable by category (e.g., DevOps, Cloud, Software Engineering) and provider/platform (e.g., AWS, GCP, Kubernetes).
Filter skills by category (including DevOps and Cloud), platform/provider (e.g., AWS), and proficiency rating via a slider or rating filter (e.g., min 4 out of 5).
Show each skill with its proficiency rating to identify skills rated 4 or 5.
Allow users to add or remove skills from a shortlist using an 'Add to shortlist' button or star icon next to each skill.
Provide a 'View shortlist' button or in-page tab/panel that switches the view to show only shortlisted skills, including the first three AWS-related skills with proficiency 4 or higher.

incoming_params.description: Optional skill category slug (e.g., 'devops' or 'cloud') to pre-filter the Skills view.
Optional platform/provider key (e.g., 'aws') used to initialize the Platform/Provider filter.
Optional minimum proficiency value to initialize the proficiency slider.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Use these skills in tailored resume' CTA

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry 'Skills'
Links from About or Resume Builder may deep-link into the Skills page with filters
Accessible with initial filters via query parameters

pages.name: Blog
pages.description: Blog listing page showing all posts with filters for tags, dates, and reading time. Supports DevOps/Kubernetes/Monitoring discovery for both reading and newsletter topic confirmation.
primary_functions: Display a list of blog post cards with title, summary, tags, published date, and estimated reading time.
Filter posts by tag (e.g., DevOps, Kubernetes, Monitoring) using a tags area.
Filter posts by published date range, including setting a start date (e.g., from 01 Jan 2023) while leaving the end date as current.
Optionally filter or expose controls related to reading time and ensure estimated reading time is visible on each post card.
Open individual blog post detail pages when a post card is clicked.
Enable users to confirm that Kubernetes and Monitoring-related posts exist before choosing newsletter topics.

incoming_params.description: Optional BlogTag.slug (e.g., 'devops', 'kubernetes', 'monitoring') to pre-filter posts by a given tag.
Optional ISO date string used to initialize the 'Published date' filter start date.
Optional minimum reading time (in minutes) used to initialize the reading time filter.
Optional maximum reading time (in minutes) used to initialize the reading time filter.
Optional full-text search query for posts.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a blog post card
Click on 'Reading list' quick access link within the blog page
Click on 'Subscribe to newsletter' CTA

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry 'Blog'
Linked from Home and Newsletter pages
Accessible with pre-selected tag/date/reading-time filters via query parameters

pages.name: Blog Post Detail
pages.description: Detail page for reading a single blog post. Allows bookmarking posts into the reading list and navigating between related posts.
primary_functions: Present the full content of a selected blog post along with metadata such as title, tags, published date, and reading time.
Provide an 'Add to reading list' or bookmark button near the title to add or remove the post from the personal reading list.
Enable users to add both short (e.g., 7 minutes or less) and long (e.g., 12 minutes or more) DevOps posts to their reading list.
Offer navigation back to the blog listing page and optional next/previous post navigation.

incoming_params.description: BlogPost.id of the post to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Back to blog' link
Click on 'Next post' navigation
Click on 'Previous post' navigation
Click on a tag chip such as 'DevOps', 'Kubernetes', or 'Monitoring'

access_methods.description: Accessed via blog_post.html?id=<BlogPost.id>
Linked from Blog listing cards, Home recent posts, and Reading List entries

pages.name: Reading List
pages.description: Bookmark/reading list page for saved blog posts. Shows all posts the recruiter has marked to read later, including the short and long DevOps posts required for the reading list task.
primary_functions: Display all blog posts that the user has added to their reading list/bookmarks.
Allow users to open any saved post in its detail view for reading.
Allow removal of posts from the reading list to keep the list current.
Serve as the destination when users click a 'Reading list' or 'Bookmarks' link from the header or menu.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a post title or 'Read' button in the reading list
Click on 'Browse all posts' link

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry 'Reading List'
Linked from Blog and Blog Post pages whenever the user wants to view saved posts

pages.name: About & Experience
pages.description: Profile and experience page including a career timeline and recruiter notes. Used for understanding the engineer's background, DevOps/SRE history, and recording the earliest DevOps/SRE year.
primary_functions: Present a personal bio, background, and high-level overview of skills and interests.
Display an 'Experience' or 'Career Timeline' section listing past roles chronologically.
Provide role-type filtering above the timeline to show or hide roles by type (e.g., DevOps, SRE, Software Engineer), allowing isolation of DevOps and SRE roles.
Allow users to scan the filtered timeline to identify the earliest start year among DevOps/SRE positions.
Expose a 'Recruiter evaluation' or 'Notes' panel linked to this page, containing an 'Earliest DevOps/SRE year' input where the user can type the four-digit year and save the notes.

incoming_params.description: Optional section anchor (e.g., 'experience') to scroll directly to the Experience timeline.
Optional comma-separated list of role types (e.g., 'devops,sre') to initialize the role-type filter.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'View DevOps projects' CTA in the experience section
Click on 'View DevOps skills' CTA
Click on 'Generate tailored resume' CTA from the About page

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry 'About'
Linked from Home and Resume Builder when more background is needed
Accessible with an optional section anchor and roleTypes filter

pages.name: Tailored Resume Builder
pages.description: Interactive tool to configure and preview a tailored resume for specific roles and locations. Supports generating a Germany-specific Senior DevOps Engineer resume focusing on CI/CD and Observability and limiting experience to the last 4 years.
primary_functions: Allow selection of a target role from a dropdown (e.g., 'Senior DevOps Engineer').
Allow entry or selection of a location/region field (e.g., 'Germany').
Provide an 'Experience timeframe' or 'Include experience from' control to limit included experience (e.g., last 4 years).
Enable selection of 'Focus skills' or 'Highlight areas' such as CI/CD and Observability so that they are emphasized in the resume.
Offer a 'Remote preference' toggle to mark openness to remote roles.
Generate or update a resume preview based on the configured role, location, timeframe, and focus skills when the user clicks 'Generate resume' or 'Update preview'.
Provide a 'Print-friendly view' or 'Full-screen resume' button that opens the tailored resume in a dedicated print view page.

incoming_params.description: Optional internal role key (e.g., 'senior_devops_engineer') used to pre-select the target role.
Optional location string (e.g., 'Germany') used to pre-fill the location field.
Optional timeframe key (e.g., 'last_4_years') to initialize the experience timeframe control.
Optional comma-separated list of focus skills (e.g., 'CI/CD,Observability') to pre-highlight in the builder.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Print-friendly view' or 'Full-screen resume' button after generating a tailored resume
Click on 'Review full experience' link from within the builder

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry 'Resume'
Linked from Home and About sections encouraging tailored resume generation
Accessible with optional pre-selected role, location, timeframe, and focus skills

pages.name: Tailored Resume Print View
pages.description: Dedicated, print-friendly view of the tailored resume configuration. Renders the most recently generated tailored resume without navigation chrome so it can be printed or exported to PDF.
primary_functions: Display the tailored resume generated from the Resume Builder in a simplified layout optimized for printing and PDF export.
Ensure the resume reflects the selected target role, location (e.g., Germany), timeframe (e.g., last 4 years), and focus skills (e.g., CI/CD, Observability).
Allow users to use browser print functionality or export to PDF without extra navigation elements.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Back to editor' link from the print view

access_methods.description: Accessed from the Tailored Resume Builder via 'Print-friendly view' button

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact form for recruiters and hiring managers to reach out. Supports composing messages that explicitly reference microservices projects and preferred interview dates.
primary_functions: Provide a form with fields for Email, Subject, and Message.
Allow users to enter a recruiter email address (e.g., 'recruiter@example-company.com').
Allow users to compose messages that reference specific project titles and dates (e.g., microservices projects) in the message textarea.
Submit the contact form through a 'Send message' or 'Submit' button.

incoming_params.description: Optional email address used to pre-fill the Email field.
Optional subject line to pre-fill the Subject field (e.g., referencing DevOps or specific projects).
Optional message template text, possibly including project titles, used to pre-fill the Message textarea.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Back to home' link after submitting or cancelling the form

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry 'Contact'
Linked from projects, resume, and about CTAs inviting recruiters to make contact
Accessible with optional pre-filled email/subject/message template parameters
Form submission handled in-place using the submitContactMessage interface

pages.name: Newsletter Subscription
pages.description: Newsletter sign-up page for ongoing updates. Lets recruiters subscribe with work email, choose Weekly frequency, and select DevOps/Cloud topics after confirming relevant Kubernetes and Monitoring content exists in the blog.
primary_functions: Provide an email input field where users can enter an address (e.g., 'hiring.manager@company.com').
Offer a frequency dropdown with options such as Weekly.
Present a 'Topics' or 'Interests' section with checkboxes for areas like DevOps and Cloud that users can select.
Submit the newsletter subscription using a 'Subscribe' or 'Sign up' button.
Serve as the destination for 'Newsletter' or 'Subscribe' links in the footer or navigation.

incoming_params.description: Optional email address used to pre-fill the subscription email field.
Optional frequency key (e.g., 'weekly') used to pre-select the frequency dropdown.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'View recent posts' link from the subscription confirmation state

access_methods.description: Footer navigation entry 'Newsletter'
Linked from Blog (e.g., 'Subscribe' CTA) and possibly Home
Accessible with optional pre-filled email and frequency via query parameters
Form submission handled in-place using the subscribeToNewsletter interface

header_links.description: Homepage introducing the engineer and key highlights.
View and filter skills, including AWS DevOps/Cloud skills.
Read DevOps, Kubernetes, and Monitoring articles.
View saved blog posts added to the reading list.
Generate a tailored resume for specific roles and locations.
View profile information and career experience timeline.
Send a message or inquiry via the contact form.

footer_links.description: Learn more about the engineer and experience history.
Browse all blog posts and articles.
Access bookmarked posts in the reading list.
Create and view a tailored resume.
Subscribe to the DevOps and Cloud-focused newsletter.
Get in touch via the contact form.
